title: Shell Script
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries POSIX shell and Bash script files used for system automation, build orchestration, and environment setup. Shell scripts are the lowest common denominator for scripting on Unix-compatible systems.

## When to use
Use `media:shell;textable;code` when a pipeline step runs or analyzes generic shell scripts — for example, a sandboxed executor running `.sh` bootstrap scripts or a static analyzer checking for unsafe patterns. For Zsh-specific scripts, prefer [Zsh Script](https://capdag.com/browse/media:zsh;textable;code); for Fish, prefer [Fish Script](https://capdag.com/browse/media:fish;textable;code).

## Notes
Both `.sh` (POSIX shell) and `.bash` (Bash-specific) extensions are covered. Scripts typically declare their interpreter via a shebang line (`#!/bin/sh` or `#!/bin/bash`), which determines runtime behavior independently of the extension. See also [Shell Script](https://capdag.com/browse/media:shell;textable;code).
